attrs.alt: External ID Custom Branding layers and assets

content.text: Custom branding in Microsoft Entra External ID lets you replace the default Microsoft sign-in page look with your product's visual identity — logo, background, colors, fonts, and text. Branding is configured at the tenant level with a default that applies to all locales, plus optional per-locale overrides for customers who use a non-English browser.
Custom branding does not require coding changes in your app. It is configured entirely in the External ID tenant and applies automatically to all user flows that are bound to your app registrations.

Set the default branding for all locales using the /organization/{id}/branding endpoint. This controls the text, colors, and layout settings. Binary assets (logo, background image) are uploaded separately to named sub-endpoints.

# Set default company branding via Graph API
# Images must be base64-encoded and sent as multipart/form-data OR
# uploaded separately using the logoRelativeUrl endpoint

# Step 1: Create the default branding object with text settings
$brandBody = @{
    signInPageText         = 'Welcome to the Contoso Customer Portal'
    usernameHintText       = 'Enter your email address'
    backgroundColor        = '#1e1e2e'
    customAccountResetCredentialsUrl = 'https://support.contoso.com/reset'
    customCannotAccessYourAccountText = 'Need help? Contact support@contoso.com'
    customForgotMyPasswordText = 'Forgot your password?'
    loginPageLayoutConfiguration = @{
        layoutTemplateType        = 'default'
        isHeaderShown             = $true
        isFooterShown             = $true
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3

# Update branding (PUT creates or replaces)
$orgId = (Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization').value[0].id
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PUT \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization/$orgId/branding" \
    -Body $brandBody -ContentType 'application/json'
Write-Host 'Default branding updated'
Image assets are uploaded via dedicated content endpoints that accept binary data. The organization ID is required; the localization ID '0' refers to the default branding.

# Upload a banner logo image to the branding configuration
# Graph API uses a streaming PUT for binary image upload
$orgId = (Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization').value[0].id

# Read the logo file
$logoBytes = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes('./contoso-logo.png')

# Upload via direct content endpoint
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PUT \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization/$orgId/branding/localizations/0/bannerLogo" \
    -InputFilePath './contoso-logo.png' \
    -ContentType 'image/png'
Write-Host 'Banner logo uploaded'

# Upload background image (same pattern)
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PUT \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization/$orgId/branding/localizations/0/backgroundImage" \
    -InputFilePath './signin-background.jpg' \
    -ContentType 'image/jpeg'
Write-Host 'Background image uploaded'

Override specific branding properties for particular locales. When a customer's browser language matches a configured locale, the locale-specific values replace the defaults for that session. Only the fields you set in the locale object are overridden — everything else inherits from the default branding.

# Create a locale-specific branding override for French users
$orgId = (Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization').value[0].id

# Create the fr-FR locale branding entry
$frBody = @{
    signInPageText   = 'Bienvenue sur le portail client Contoso'
    usernameHintText = 'Entrez votre adresse e-mail'
    customCannotAccessYourAccountText = 'Besoin d aide? Contactez support@contoso.com'
} | ConvertTo-Json

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PUT \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization/$orgId/branding/localizations/fr-FR" \
    -Body $frBody -ContentType 'application/json'

# Upload French-language logo for fr-FR locale
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PUT \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization/$orgId/branding/localizations/fr-FR/bannerLogo" \
    -InputFilePath './contoso-logo-fr.png' \
    -ContentType 'image/png'
Write-Host 'French locale branding configured'

Custom CSS is injected into the sign-in page HTML and lets you restyle elements that are not exposed through the branding API — fonts, button colors, panel layout, and visual effects. JavaScript is blocked by Content Security Policy; use only CSS. Reference external assets (fonts, background images) via absolute HTTPS URLs hosted on your own CDN.

Replace the default ciamlogin.com URL with your own domain. Customers see login.yourapp.com throughout the authentication flow, which reinforces your brand and hides the Microsoft infrastructure from the user experience.

# Custom domain hides ciamlogin.com from customers
# Customers see: https://login.contoso.com/... instead of https://contoso.ciamlogin.com/...

# Step 1: Add CNAME in DNS registrar
# login.contoso.com → contoso.ciamlogin.com

# Step 2: Register the custom domain in the External ID tenant
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/domains' \
    -Body (@{ id = 'login.contoso.com' } | ConvertTo-Json) \
    -ContentType 'application/json'

# Step 3: Get verification DNS records
$records = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/domains/login.contoso.com/verificationDnsRecords'
$records.value | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "Add TXT record: $($_.text) at: $($_.label)"
}

# Step 4: Verify after adding the TXT record
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/domains/login.contoso.com/verify'
Write-Host 'Domain verified. Update app redirect URIs to use login.contoso.com'

# Step 5: Update app registration redirect URIs
$appId = "your-app-client-id"
$oldRedirect = "https://contoso.ciamlogin.com/contoso.onmicrosoft.com/oauth2/authresp"
$newRedirect = "https://login.contoso.com/contoso.onmicrosoft.com/oauth2/authresp"

$app = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/applications?`$filter=appId eq '$appId'"
$appObjId = $app.value[0].id
$appObj = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/applications/$appObjId"
$newUris = ($appObj.web.redirectUris | Where-Object { $_ -ne $oldRedirect }) + $newRedirect
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PATCH \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/applications/$appObjId" \
    -Body (@{ web = @{ redirectUris = $newUris } } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3) \
    -ContentType 'application/json'
Write-Host 'App redirect URIs updated to use custom domain'
Read the current branding configuration programmatically to audit what is set and verify locale overrides before deploying to production.

# Read current branding configuration
$orgId = (Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization').value[0].id

# Get default branding
$branding = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization/$orgId/branding"
Write-Host "Sign-in text: $($branding.signInPageText)"
Write-Host "Background color: $($branding.backgroundColor)"

# List all locale overrides
$localizations = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET \
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization/$orgId/branding/localizations"
$localizations.value | ForEach-Object {
    Write-Host "Locale: $($_.id) | Text: $($_.signInPageText)"
}
External ID supports branding localization for 36 languages. The browser's Accept-Language header determines which locale to apply. If no match is found, the default branding is used.
fr-FR, de-DE, es-ES, it-IT, nl-NL, pt-PT, pl-PL, cs-CZ
ja-JP, zh-CN, zh-TW, ko-KR, th-TH, vi-VN
ru-RU, uk-UA, ro-RO, hu-HU, bg-BG, hr-HR, sk-SK, sl-SI
Use a transparent background on your banner logo — the sign-in page background shows through and a white logo box looks inconsistent.
Test on both light and dark OS themes; customers on Windows and macOS use dark mode, and a white background in your logo will stand out poorly.
Keep custom CSS minimal — Microsoft updates the sign-in page HTML periodically and overly specific CSS selectors break silently.
Host custom fonts and CSS background images on a CDN with a permissive CORS policy so the browser can load them from the ciamlogin.com or custom domain origin.
Set a backgroundColor that matches your background image's dominant color so there's no flash of unstyled content while the image loads.
Verify branding in an incognito browser window to see it without cached assets from previous testing.
All branding operations are available through the Microsoft Graph API. The following examples use PowerShell with the Microsoft.Graph module.

Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Organization.ReadWrite.All'

# Get the current default organizational branding
$branding = Get-MgOrganizationBranding -OrganizationId (Get-MgOrganization).Id
$branding | Select-Object SignInPageText, UsernameHintText, BackgroundColor

# List all locale-specific branding overrides
$locales = Get-MgOrganizationBrandingLocalization -OrganizationId (Get-MgOrganization).Id
foreach ($loc in $locales) {
    Write-Host "Locale: $($loc.Id) | Sign-in text: $($loc.SignInPageText)"
}
Updating Sign-In Page Text and Background Color

$orgId = (Get-MgOrganization).Id

# Update the default branding sign-in page text and background
Update-MgOrganizationBranding -OrganizationId $orgId -BodyParameter @{
    SignInPageText = 'Welcome to Contoso Identity Portal'
    UsernameHintText = 'user@contoso.com'
    BackgroundColor = '#1a1a2e'
    SquareLogoRelativeUrl = $null   # retains existing if null
}
Write-Host 'Default branding text and color updated.'
Uploading a Banner Logo via Graph Binary Endpoint

$orgId = (Get-MgOrganization).Id
$logoPath = './assets/contoso-banner-logo.png'
$logoBytes = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes($logoPath)

# PUT the binary content to the bannerLogo sub-resource
Invoke-MgGraphRequest `
    -Method PUT `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization/$orgId/branding/bannerLogo" `
    -Body $logoBytes `
    -ContentType 'image/png'

Write-Host 'Banner logo uploaded successfully.'
Infrastructure-as-Code teams can manage branding assets declaratively using the AzureAD (Entra) Terraform provider, ensuring branding is version-controlled alongside tenant configuration.
Auditing Branding Changes with Entra Audit Logs
All branding modifications are recorded in the Microsoft Entra audit log under the 'Authentication Methods' category. Use KQL queries in Log Analytics to track changes over time.

// Audit log entries for organizational branding changes
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where Category == 'Authentication Methods'
| where OperationName has_any ('Update organizational branding', 'Set organizational branding', 'Delete organizational branding')
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    OperationName,
    InitiatedBy = InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName,
    TargetResources = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName),
    Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc

AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName has 'localization'
| extend LocaleId = tostring(TargetResources[0].id)
| extend Actor = InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName
| summarize ChangeCount = count() by LocaleId, Actor, bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
Logo appears blurry on high-DPI screens
Image uploaded at 1x resolution without retina variant
Upload 2x resolution PNG; scale dimensions but keep file < 36 KB
Image > 1920×1080 or > 1.8 MB; mobile uses backgroundColor instead
Resize to ≤1920×1080, optimize with TinyPNG to < 1.8 MB
CSS file > 25 KB or references external fonts not on the allowlist
Inline fonts as base64 data URIs; keep file under 25 KB
Use BCP 47 format; check supported locales table for exact codes
Guest invited from a tenant with no passthrough branding policy
Enable 'Show home realm branding' in CTAS inbound settings
Wait 15 minutes after update; test in incognito / different browser
To reset branding to Microsoft defaults, delete the branding configuration via the Graph API. This is non-reversible without re-uploading assets.

$orgId = (Get-MgOrganization).Id

# Remove a specific locale branding override
$localeToRemove = 'fr-FR'
Remove-MgOrganizationBrandingLocalization `
    -OrganizationId $orgId `
    -OrganizationBrandingLocalizationId $localeToRemove
Write-Host "Locale branding '$localeToRemove' removed."

# To fully reset default branding to Microsoft defaults:
# DELETE https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization/{id}/branding
Invoke-MgGraphRequest `
    -Method DELETE `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization/$orgId/branding"
Write-Host 'Default branding reset to Microsoft defaults.'
Custom branding replaces the Microsoft sign-in page appearance with your product's logo, background, colors, and text.
Two layers: a default branding (all locales) and per-locale overrides (36 supported languages).
Binary assets (logo, background) are uploaded to dedicated content sub-endpoints; text settings use JSON PATCH/PUT.
Custom CSS lets you restyle elements not exposed in the branding API — but JavaScript is blocked by CSP.
A custom domain (login.yourapp.com via CNAME) hides the ciamlogin.com URL from customers and reinforces brand consistency.
Test branding in incognito, on both light and dark OS themes, and across the locales you configure.
